title: Stomatanthes polycephalus
uses: Stomatanthes polycephalus, a plant species native to Brazil, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various health issues. Research suggests that the plant's bioactive compounds may also possess anti-inflammatory and antinociceptive effects, making it a subject of interest for further investigation. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional medicine in Brazil to treat ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and to validate its safety for use in humans.
taxonomy: Stomatanthes polycephalus is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it in the order Asterales. Further, it is grouped into the subfamily Vernonioideae and then into the tribe Vernonieae. The genus Stomatanthes is part of this tribe and is native to South America. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro: Stomatanthes polycephalus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a member of the daisy or sunflower family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Stomatanthes polycephalus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
